text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

---------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen ID:       SP-XXXX-XXXXX
Accession #:       [Accession assigned on receipt]
Patient:           Tran, Margaret
Date of Birth:     [DOB on file]
Ordering Provider: [Requesting Pulmonologist / Interventional Radiology]
Attending Pathologist: [Attending Pathologist on file]
Resident/Fellow:   [Trainee on file]

---------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL HISTORY (AS SUBMITTED)
---------------------------------------------------------------------
62-year-old female. Former smoker (12 pack-years, quit approximately 18 years prior). Known right lower lobe spiculated mass measuring 4.8 cm on CT chest with ipsilateral mediastinal and subcarinal lymphadenopathy (stations 4R and 7). PET/CT demonstrates FDG-avid primary mass (SUVmax 14.2) and hypermetabolic mediastinal nodes without evidence of distant metastatic disease. Brain MRI negative for intracranial metastases. Procedure performed for tissue diagnosis and molecular testing.

Past medical history: Hypertension, hypothyroidism (on levothyroxine).

---------------------------------------------------------------------
PROCEDURE / SPECIMEN TYPE
---------------------------------------------------------------------
Procedure:         CT-guided transthoracic core needle biopsy, right lower lobe mass
Specimen type:     Core needle biopsy (18-gauge), multiple cores submitted
Number of passes:  3 passes performed under CT fluoroscopic guidance
Site:              Right lower lobe pulmonary mass, peripheral (posterior segment)
Clinical indication: New right lower lobe mass with mediastinal adenopathy, rule out malignancy

---------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN RECEIPT AND GROSS PATHOLOGY
---------------------------------------------------------------------
Received fresh in formalin-fixed state. Specimen labeled with patient name and specimen site. Consists of three core needle biopsy fragments, each tan-white to pale gray in color, firm in consistency, ranging from 0.9 to 1.3 cm in length and approximately 1 mm in greatest diameter. Aggregate core length: approximately 3.2 cm. No grossly necrotic or hemorrhagic areas identified. All material submitted entirely in one cassette for permanent sections, with one additional core held in RNAlater (Thermo Fisher) for potential molecular studies.

Representative sections submitted in full. Material sufficient for H&E evaluation and ancillary studies.

---------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
---------------------------------------------------------------------
Histologic sections demonstrate a moderately differentiated invasive pulmonary adenocarcinoma. The tumor is characterized by a mixed growth pattern with acinar architecture predominating (approximately 60%) and a secondary lepidic component (approximately 30%). A minor solid component is present (<10%) without evidence of mucin production. The acinar glands are lined by cuboidal to columnar neoplastic epithelial cells with vesicular nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and moderate to abundant pale eosinophilic cytoplasm. Mitotic figures are present at approximately 4-5 per 10 high-power fields. No atypical mitoses identified. The lepidic areas demonstrate neoplastic cells spreading along intact alveolar walls without destruction of alveolar architecture. No extensive central necrosis identified; focal comedonecrosis within acinar lumens present in one core fragment.

The background lung parenchyma adjacent to the tumor shows mild chronic inflammation and a mild degree of peribronchiolar fibrosis. No granulomatous inflammation identified. No neuroendocrine features (trabecular/nested pattern, salt-and-pepper chromatin, or Azzopardi effect) are present. Vascular invasion: present, focal, in one of three cores. Perineural invasion: not identified.

Marcus staining of an additional slide confirms presence of mucin within approximately 5% of glandular lumens (mucicarmine, focal weak positivity).

---------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY
---------------------------------------------------------------------
The following immunohistochemical stains were performed on 4-micron formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) sections with appropriate positive and negative controls:

  TTF-1 (SP141 clone, Ventana):           POSITIVE — strong, diffuse nuclear staining, >90% of tumor cells
  Napsin A (polyclonal, Leica):            POSITIVE — moderate to strong granular cytoplasmic staining, ~80% of tumor cells
  CK7 (OV-TL 12/30, Dako):               POSITIVE — diffuse cytoplasmic staining
  CK20 (Ks20.8, Dako):                    NEGATIVE
  p40 (BC28, Biocare):                    NEGATIVE — confirms absence of squamous differentiation
  p63 (4A4, Dako):                        NEGATIVE
  Synaptophysin (MRQ-40, Cell Marque):    NEGATIVE — no neuroendocrine differentiation
  Chromogranin A (LK2H10, Ventana):       NEGATIVE
  CD56 (123C3, Leica):                    NEGATIVE

IHC profile (TTF-1+, Napsin A+, CK7+, CK20-, p40-) is consistent with a primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma and argues against metastatic adenocarcinoma from the colon, breast, or other extrapulmonary site.

---------------------------------------------------------------------
PD-L1 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY
---------------------------------------------------------------------
Assay:             PD-L1 IHC 22C3 pharmDx (Dako/Agilent) — FDA-approved companion diagnostic assay for NSCLC
Scored by:         Attending Pathologist (board-certified, PD-L1 trained)
Quality control:   Positive and negative internal and external controls satisfactory

Tumor Proportion Score (TPS): 15%
  -- Defined as the percentage of viable tumor cells demonstrating partial or complete membrane staining at any intensity.
  -- TPS 15%: Low expressors (1-49%), interpretation per scoring guidelines.

Note: CPS (combined positive score) not formally reported for this indication; TPS is the validated scoring parameter for NSCLC by 22C3 assay.

---------------------------------------------------------------------
MOLECULAR / ANCILLARY STUDIES
---------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen adequacy for molecular testing confirmed by the performing pathologist. Tumor cellularity is estimated at 40-50% of submitted material, exceeding the minimum threshold required for comprehensive NGS analysis.

The following molecular testing has been ordered or is pending at time of this report:
  - Reflex comprehensive next-generation sequencing (solid tumor panel, tumor-normal paired): ORDERED — see separate NGS report when available
  - ALK FISH (Vysis LSI ALK Break-Apart FISH Probe Kit): ORDERED — results pending
  - ROS1 FISH: ORDERED — results pending

Note: Molecular results will be reported as an addendum to this pathology report upon completion.

---------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS
---------------------------------------------------------------------
1. RIGHT LOWER LOBE, PULMONARY MASS — CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:

   ADENOCARCINOMA, MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED

   - Histologic pattern: Mixed acinar (predominant) and lepidic growth
   - Differentiation: Moderate (Grade 2)
   - IHC profile: TTF-1+, Napsin A+, CK7+, CK20-, p40- — consistent with primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma
   - PD-L1 TPS: 15% (22C3 assay; low expressors, 1-49% range)
   - Vascular invasion: present, focal
   - Specimen adequacy: Adequate for molecular testing
   - Molecular studies: Pending (see NGS addendum)

CORRELATION WITH CLINICAL AND RADIOGRAPHIC FINDINGS RECOMMENDED

---------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
---------------------------------------------------------------------
The morphologic and immunophenotypic features are diagnostic of a primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma. The mixed acinar-predominant and lepidic growth pattern, together with strong TTF-1 and Napsin A positivity, are characteristic of lung adenocarcinoma. Squamous cell carcinoma and large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma are excluded by negative p40, p63, synaptophysin, chromogranin, and CD56. The absence of CK20 expression argues against a primary colorectal or urothelial origin. The relatively low PD-L1 TPS of 15% is noted and falls in the low-expression category; however, definitive therapeutic implications should be considered in conjunction with molecular profiling results.

All FFPE material from this biopsy, including unstained sections and residual block, has been retained per departmental protocol. Additional material may be available upon request for confirmatory or research purposes.

---------------------------------------------------------------------
ELECTRONIC SIGN-OUT
---------------------------------------------------------------------
Attending Pathologist: [Electronically signed — name and credentials on file]
Date/Time of Final Sign-Out: [Date/time assigned programmatically]
Status: FINAL

This report has been electronically signed and is considered final. Amendments, if any, will be issued as an addendum.

---------------------------------------------------------------------
